input: user-item interaction graph and knowledge graph (heterogeneous graph with entities and relations)
output: ranked list of items per user
backbone: relational graph convolutional neural network (R-GCN) with graph attention
loss: BPR + multiple adaptive contrast-loss optimization functions (including hard negative sampling)
key_idea: leverage relational graph convolution and contrastive learning on knowledge subgraphs to mitigate cold start and noise
datasets: real public datasets (not specified in abstract)
